summary: Elder Clark G. Gilbert teaches that Jesus Christ, our Redeemer and Repairer, lovingly calls all who feel distant—because of unbelonging, inadequacy, doubt, or tradition—to come home through His grace and covenant path.
haiku:
Soft call through the storm
A saved seat glows in His light
Weary hearts come home
key_points: Christ’s grace makes up the difference when we feel inadequate or unworthy.
Those who feel they do not belong, who doubt, or who are held back by traditions can still be restored to light, love, and joy through the Savior.
Members can help others come home by keeping covenants, showing patient love, and making room for them.
scriptures_and_gospel_principles: Jesus Christ as Redeemer and Repairer
The covenant path and God’s merciful patience
Repentance and returning to the Savior
The role of the Holy Ghost in discerning truth
Temple covenants, endowment, and sealing blessings
President Russell M. Nelson’s teachings on God never tiring in His efforts to help His covenant people
President Dallin H. Oaks’s teaching that the journey home begins by reanchoring on the Savior
questions_to_ponder: When have I felt the Savior inviting me to come home?
What barriers—such as feeling I do not belong, not measuring up, doubt, or tradition—might keep me or others from drawing closer to Christ?
How can I better trust that Christ will make up the difference as I do my best?
Who around me may need to see a light signaling that there is a place for them?
How can keeping my covenants help me support loved ones who are struggling spiritually?
